requirements: validation, focus, cleanup, and form participation

gluon.tool: no source-located diagnostic observed in the retained language-server fixture

vue.tool: none observed in the retained fixture checks

react.tool: none observed in the retained fixture checks

cleanupEvidence: tests/dx-stateful-form-control.spec.ts records class disconnect, functional owner cleanup, Vue onUnmounted, and React useEffect disposal after host removal
tests/memory-retention.spec.ts verifies 30 functional connect/disconnect cleanup cycles
findings: The functional Gluon fixture removes 28 non-empty authored lines and all three repeated instance property declarations relative to the retained Gluon class fixture.
The complete autonomous-control fixtures contain 77 non-empty authored lines for functional Gluon, 125 for Vue, and 120 for React.
The framework-local view sections contain 66 non-empty lines for functional Gluon, 27 for Vue, and 20 for React; Vue and React are more concise on this narrower component-only measure.
The React TSX lane requires the root TypeScript jsx=react-jsx compiler option; the Gluon and Vue TypeScript fixtures do not consume that transform.
Vue and React require retained autonomous Custom Element, ElementInternals, SSR-hydration, and cleanup bridge code to satisfy the same plain-HTML and form-control boundary.
All four retained controls pass the same browser behavior assertions; all four produce retained server markup for the same quantity and computed total.

verifiedGluonDisadvantages.fact: The functional Gluon component section is 39 lines longer than Vue's view section and 46 lines longer than React's view section.
verifiedGluonDisadvantages.scope: Framework-local view code only; excludes the Vue and React autonomous Custom Element bridges.

verifiedGluonDisadvantages.fact: Both Gluon fixtures register a hyphenated autonomous Custom Element name in the global CustomElementRegistry; the retained Vue and React view components do not require that registry until their plain-HTML bridges are added.
verifiedGluonDisadvantages.scope: Local component reuse before applying the required plain-HTML boundary.

verifiedGluonDisadvantages.fact: The retained language-server test diagnoses unknown properties and events but does not assert a source-located diagnostic for an unknown named light-DOM slot.
verifiedGluonDisadvantages.scope: Current functional Custom Element language-tooling evidence.

limitations: This is an implementation-slice fixture, not a completed benchmark run.
It does not contain all 21 framework-task results required by the parent schema.
No human usability pass was run; no readability, win, tie, loss, usability, or general DX-superiority claim is supported.
The authored-line measures exclude blank lines and the two DX range marker comments; they do not measure maintenance time or cognitive effort.
